translated: So, from here, I'd like to talk about the illustrations.
I'll start by reading a message.
This one is from Meshi.
Thank you so much.
"Hello, Sato-san."
"I'm always having a great time playing ProSeka."
"The 4-star illustrations this time, especially Mizuki's, were so good that I went all out on the gacha..."
"...and I managed to get them!"
"Just like in 'Secret Distance' or 'Our Escape to the Unknown'..."
"'Michi wa Izuko'..."
"...the post-trained illustration features Mizuki in a space with a torso."
"A torso is like a mannequin for clothes, which often appears in Mizuki's art."
It's right here...
Right here.
This is called a torso.
"Reaching out to an unbinding tomorrow... the torso."
"It's an illustration with Mizuki in a space filled with torsos."
"The torso space usually represented Mizuki's dark feelings,"
"but this time, they're looking forward after feeling the warmth of family and friends."
"Seeing the space reflect Mizuki's bright and cheerful state of mind,"
"I was so happy to see how much their heart has changed."
"I can't thank the staff enough for creating such a wonderful scenario,"
"and the voice actors for giving the characters such depth and expression."
"Thank you for always creating such wonderful events."
"Please take care of yourselves and keep up the good work."
"I'll be rooting for you as a fellow Toufu," they wrote.
Meshi, thank you so much.
The colors in these illustrations are quite rare for Niigo.
We've had pale colors before, but seeing Niigo bathed in sunlight...
Well, they don't really go outside much.
Seeing Niigo during the day is rare, and I felt this specific art style was quite unique.
Everyone's cards were great, not just Mizuki's, both pre-trained and post-trained.
It makes me realize what amazing artists we have.
I'm really grateful. We also received comments from the illustration team about the outfits,
so I'd like to share those as well.
They mentioned that "pure brightness" is quite rare for them.
"The story was inspired by Mizuki, who was accepted by Niigo, wanting to help others who have similar worries..."
"...or who can't love themselves, by making clothes for them."
"It also draws from the story of Mizuki thinking about their future career in fashion."
"To express the image of a future they've finally started to see,"
"we chose a simple and pure one-piece design with few elements."
"As a highlight, we included the yellow daisy embroidery used in Mizuki's new unit costume..."
"...to convey the message of 'being yourself'."
"This resulted in a simple outfit design unique to Mizuki."
"To match the song's calm, warm impression and Mizuki's faint hope for the future,"
"we used a palette of soft, gentle colors," they said.
It's true, everyone's cards have a very soft color scheme this time.
And did everyone notice the daisy? Did you see it?
There's a daisy in there.
I see comments about the daisy now.
"The blue traffic light was striking."
That's true.
Mizuki was born in summer, on August 2... Happy Birthday!
I didn't mean to say it so casually, but Happy Birthday.
Since it's summer, I feel like Mizuki hasn't had many summer-like illustrations until now.
They usually wear long sleeves or clothes that cover their skin.
Though there have been T-shirts and things like that.
So, seeing an illustration that feels like summer the moment you look at it is really nice.
With that said, let's take a look at the card illustrations together.
I'm on the monitor right now. On everyone's monitor.
I believe so, anyway.
Let's start with Mizuki's pre-trained card.
This illustration...
Their sister is looking at Mizuki with such a happy smile. In the New Year's event,
it really reminds me of Yuuki-san giving a ribbon to a young Mizuki.
And Mizuki's expression while showing her the outfit is just wonderful.
I can't believe this is a pre-trained card.
Seriously.
I love the atmosphere here.
You can see the torso in the background.
Now, let's look at the post-trained version.
It's very...
I've always felt that butterflies and ribbons are very symbolic for Mizuki.
